Vyzdvihnúť> Platia zmluvné podmienky  Dovozné poplatky sa budú vzťahovať na všetky položky uvedené v tomto e-maile alebo v rámci tejto propagačnej akcie, ktoré sa odosielajú z krajín mimo EÚ. Nakúpte za viac ako 5 € (okrem položiek odosielaných z lokálneho skladu) a získajte zľavnen

76219.subject: 🦞 Agent Swarm Breaches Hugging Face
76219.body: 96 🦞 Agent Swarm Breaches Hugging Face @media only screen and (max-width: 620px) { table.body { width: 100%; min-width: 100%; } table.body .content { padding: 0 !important; } table.body .container { padding: 0 !important; width: 100% !important; } table.body .main { border-spacing: 10px 0 !important; border-left-width: 0 !important; border-radius: 0 !important; border-right-width: 0 !important; } table.body .img-responsive { height: auto !important; max-width: 100% !important; width: auto !important; } } @media all { .ExternalClass { width: 100%; } .ExternalClass, .ExternalClass p, .ExternalClass span, .ExternalClass font, .ExternalClass td, .ExternalClass div { line-height: 100%; } .apple-link a { color: inherit !important; font-family: inherit !important; font-size: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: inherit !important; text-decoration: none !important; } #MessageViewBody a { color: inherit; text-decoration: none; font-size: inherit; font-family: inherit; font-weight: inherit; line-height: inherit; } } @media only screen and (max-width: 620px) { table[class=body] h1 { font-size: 28px !important; margin-bottom: 10px !important; } table[class=body] p, table[class=body] ul, table[class=body] ol, table[class=body] td, table[class=body] span, table[class=body] a { font-size: 16px !important; } table[class=body] .wrapper, table[class=body] .article { padding: 10px !important; } table[class=body] .content { padding: 0 !important; } table[class=body] .container { padding: 0 !important; width: 100% !important; } table[class=body] .main { border-left-width: 0 !important; border-radius: 0 !important; border-right-width: 0 !important; } table[class=body] .btn table { width: 100% !important; } table[class=body] .btn a { width: 100% !important; } table[class=body] .img-responsive { height: auto !important; max-width: 100% !important; width: auto !important; } table[class=body] p[class=small], table[class=body] a[class=small] { font-size: 11px !important; } } .kg-nft-link { display: block; text-decoration: none !important; color: #000000 !important; font-family: inherit !important; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.3; padding-top: 4px; padding-right: 20px; padding-left: 20px; padding-bottom: 4px; } .kg-twitter-link { display: block; text-decoration: none !important; color: #000000 !important; font-family: inherit !important; font-size: 15px; padding: 8px; line-height: 1.3; } .kg-cta-link-accent .kg-cta-sponsor-label a { color: #ff4141 !important; } .kg-cta-text a:not(.kg-cta-link-accent .kg-cta-text a) { color: #000000; text-decoration: none; } .kg-cta-link-accent .kg-cta-text a { color: #ff4141 !important; } @media only screen and (max-width: 620px) { table.body .kg-bookmark-card { width: 90vw; } table.body .kg-bookmark-thumbnail { display: none !important; } table.body .kg-bookmark-metadata span { font-size: 13px !important; } table.body .kg-embed-card { max-width: 90vw !important; } table.body .kg-cta-card { padding: 0 20px; }

Fear and Greed is at
34
(Fear)
and Bitcoin is at
$64K
this morning. Bitcoin held its ground over the weekend, trading
in a tight range between $63.5K and $64.5K as volume stayed thin
ahead of a pivotal week. The Fed's rate decision on July 29 is
now the market's focal point.

Positioning data tells the real story this Monday. Large traders
are making a specific, time-bound bet on where BTC goes next.
Meanwhile FTX creditors are days away from another major payout,
and stablecoin regulation just missed its own deadline.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

FTX Is Paying Out Billions to Creditors Again This Month

The FTX Recovery Trust will distribute approximately $900
million to creditors on July 31, its fifth payout since the
exchange's November 2022 collapse.

Total distributions will reach roughly $10 billion once this
round completes. Payments will go out through BitGo, Kraken, or
Payoneer within one to three business days.

What was the record date creditors needed to meet to be
eligible?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/JBXqLJFcFTmcBlQPDjqXTg~~/AAQRxRA~/nldvwdh61IwcnhHVhlo_sX11eTXxdmwoqOJaf5NvA7iBxL8EoMw-Il3U6xJJrwc8hfLdKhZtIWHRwzuCBnKaDjMox-DXfOnpEfVlmb5N8CSdvrr3zudVhoLUDIUNJXRNjEmkb9Qf00vJE7qyARbiikYcJWOftAonuoHxf3MI7aY~ )
   France Just Ordered ISPs to Block Polymarket

France's gambling regulator ordered internet service providers
to block Polymark

Enjoying this newsletter? Get it in your inbox every weekend, as well as our daily round-up of The Economist’s best journalism, by signing up for free here. The war in Vietnam took six years to become as hated by Americans as the Iran war has become in six months. Net approval for Donald Trump’s war among Democratic voters is a solidly abject -84%. But here’s a surprise: for the first time, many more than half of his supporters approve only “somewhat” of how he’s handling his job, according to a Washington Post/Ipsos poll. Elsewhere: China’s young billionaires have made their wealth from video games, tea, AI and Labubus. This generation of tycoons is China’s most global—and its most enigmatic. Stephanie Studer, Senior digital editor What to expect in the week ahead: ▸ Andy Burnham is sworn in as Britain’s new prime minister today. He is expected to announce his cabinet. Investors have been nervously waiting for his pick for chancellor. Shabana Mahmood, the current home secretary, has reportedly emerged as a frontrunner. ▸ Joseph Aoun, Lebanon’s president, will meet Donald Trump for the first time on Tuesday. It has been nearly 20 years since a Lebanese head of state has visited the White House. Mr Aoun will reportedly present a plan on how to disarm Hizbullah, a militant group in Lebanon, and secure Israel’s withdrawal from the country. ▸ Alphabet reports its second-quarter earnings on Wednesday. The company’s market value rose by $2trn over 12 months, and in June it issued an $85bn share offering, the biggest-ever corporate equity-raising exercise. But its results come amid investor concerns about AI spending. Along with other tech giants, Alphabet’s stock fell in June. Editor’s picks The Iran war is America’s least popular since polls began Not far from Martin Luther King’s grey stone memorial in Washington is a big golden cup honouring Donald Trump. The “Iran War Participation Trophy”, erected by anonymous satirists, suggests that on the battlefield it is not the winning that counts but the taking part. “While some concern themselves with…measurable outcomes, President Trump demonstrated the courage to participate,” reads the inscription. 76215.subject: How to Build an AI Monopoly: The New Rules of Startup Strategy 🦄
76215.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/how-to-build-an-ai-monopoly

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 397k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
We are living through the greatest value creation event in the history of technology - and most of the people building it will capture none of the value.
And that is not pessimism. It is pure arithmetic. U.S. private AI investment hit $285.9 billion in 2025, more than 23 times China’s, with 1,953 newly funded AI startups in the United States alone, and enterprise spending on generative AI more than tripled, from $11.5 billion to $37 billion (Stanford HAI AI Index 2026, Menlo Ventures). At the same time, the cost of intelligence is collapsing. Anthropic’s Opus-class flagship [ https://substack.com/redirect/96177bc0-14d5-4253-8762-cf44c75a2e92?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] now sells for a third of its 2025 price, $5 per million input tokens against $15 in the Opus 4.1 era, the cost of matching any fixed level of model performance is falling by up to two orders of magnitude a year, and open-weight models like GLM-5.2 [ https://substack.com/redirect/f89e2c3e-16de-440d-baed-1498e93cef9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] trail the closed frontier by roughly just four months.
In other words, anyone can now build an impressive AI product in a weekend. Which means an impressive AI product is worth almost nothing.
Here is the defining trap of the AI age: the technology massively increases the value being created while competition relentlessly drives the value captured toward zero. Thousands of founders are about to learn, expensively, that a great demo and a fine-tuned model in a crowded category add up to a company worth approximately nothing.
But there is a framework for escaping this trap. It was laid out a decade ago in a 40-minute Stanford lecture that has quietly become the most important strategy text of the AI era, and almost everyone building in AI today is violating its every principle.
What follows moves in three steps, each building on the last:
The framework itself: the value-capture formula, the two kinds of businesses, the lies companies tell about their markets, and the psychology that pulls smart founders into unwinnable fights.
The translation to AI in 2026: commoditizing models, thin-wrapper economics, agents, vertical AI, and the new moats of context, memory, and evals.
The tools for Monday morning: twelve concrete plays, a monopoly scorecard, a “kill the idea” checklist, seven questions every AI founder must answer, and a 90-day operating plan.
Bonus: the entire playbook is codified as an installable Claude Skill, built to work alongside the twelve-skill startup AI OS [ https://substack.com/redirect/edb4d567-09b2-41a0-8da6-fdd72f9a5438?
